notes: Bronze DQ rules for BEA Regional Price Parities (RPP) by state, All Items, 2024. 51 rows (50 states + DC). Current load has 8 spec-verified values and 43 primary-agent estimates (source_method=csv_cache). All thresholds are designed to hold BOTH today (estimates-in-place) AND after a future live BEA API refresh replaces the estimates. Key design constraints per EDA: (1) rpp_all_items has only 50 distinct values because Iowa and Oklahoma legitimately tie at 87.8 -- NO uniqueness rule on rpp_all_items. (2) source_method must use IN ('bea_api','csv_cache') -- current load is 100% csv_cache but future loads will be bea_api. (3) ingested_at and load_date are load-batch stamps, not per-row event time -- no rules pinning them to exact values.

rules.name: Row count exactly 51 (50 states + DC)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.bea_rpp) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The BEA SARPP table at LineCode=1 (All Items), Year=2024, GeoFips=STATE returns exactly 51 geographic entities: 50 U.S. states plus the District of Columbia. Any other count indicates the ingestor dropped rows, double-loaded, or picked up metro-level GeoFips values.
rules.evidence: EDA 'Key Findings': 'Row count is exactly 51 as required by the spec. No extras, no duplicates.' EDA 'Edge Cases' table: 'Exact row count = 51, 51/51, 100%, P0 hard-equal row count rule. Both estimate and verified states share this constraint.' Spec DQ Rules (Bronze): 'Row count: exactly 51 (P0)'. Threshold: count = 51 exactly.

rules.name: rpp_all_items non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE rpp_all_items IS NULL
rules.description: rpp_all_items is the single core measure of this reference table. Every row must have a numeric value; null means a parse failure in the ingestor (BEA DataValue field arrived as a string that could not be coerced to float).
rules.evidence: EDA 'rpp_all_items' field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. EDA 'Edge Cases': 'rpp_all_items non-null, 51/51, 100%, P0 not-null rule'. Spec: 'RPP non-null: 100% (P0)'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: rpp_all_items in range [80.0, 130.0]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE rpp_all_items < 80.0 OR rpp_all_items > 130.0
rules.description: BEA's historical range of state-level All Items RPPs over the last decade has been ~85-113. The [80.0, 130.0] guardrail gives ~5 point headroom below the historical floor and ~17 point headroom above the historical ceiling. Any value outside this range signals a unit error, decimal-point parse error, or a non-state GeoFips leaking into the load.
rules.evidence: EDA 'Key Findings': 'RPP range [86.90, 110.70] is safely inside the spec DQ guardrails of [80.0, 130.0].' EDA 'Edge Cases': 'rpp_all_items within [86.90, 110.70] observed, 51/51, 100%, P0 range [80.0, 130.0] as spec says is safe with generous headroom in both directions (~7 pts margin below, ~19 pts above). No real BEA release in the last decade has fallen outside this.' This rule is estimate-tolerant and refresh-tolerant. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: geo_fips uniqueness (primary key)
rules.sql: SELECT geo_fips, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM bronze.bea_rpp GROUP BY geo_fips HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: geo_fips is the declared dedup grain (one row per state FIPS). Any duplicate indicates a dedup failure in the ingestor or a double-append to the Iceberg table.
rules.evidence: EDA 'geo_fips' field profile: 'Cardinality: 51 distinct values (100% unique -- primary key candidate)'. Spec: 'Dedup grain: [geo_fips]'. Spec DQ Rules: 'geo_fips uniqueness (P0)'. Threshold: 0 duplicate geo_fips values.

rules.name: geo_name non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE geo_name IS NULL
rules.description: geo_name is the human-readable state/DC name. Required by schema. Null indicates a BEA API response parse failure.
rules.evidence: EDA 'geo_name' field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51). Cardinality: 51 distinct values (100% unique).' Spec: 'geo_name non-null: 100% (P0)'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: data_year equals 2024
rules.description: This load targets the BEA SARPP Year=2024 release (published February 2026). Every row must carry data_year=2024. On annual refresh, bump this rule to the new target year.
rules.evidence: EDA 'data_year' field profile: 'Cardinality: 1 distinct value (2024). Distribution: All 51 rows = 2024.' EDA 'Edge Cases': 'data_year == 2024, 51/51, 100%, P0 constant-equality rule -- revisit on annual refresh'. Domain context: 'Current load is data_year = 2024 (released February 2026).' Threshold: 0 rows with data_year != 2024.

rules.name: California (FIPS 06) RPP between 108.0 and 115.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE geo_fips = '06' AND (rpp_all_items < 108.0 OR rpp_all_items > 115.0)
rules.description: Sanity spot-check against a known high-cost state. California's RPP has ranged ~108.5-111.0 over the past 5 BEA publications. The [108.0, 115.0] window covers that history with generous upside headroom. A value outside this window means the row for CA is stale, mis-keyed, or corrupted.
rules.evidence: EDA 'Estimated vs. Verified' table: California at FIPS 06 is spec-verified at 110.7 (matches BEA release exactly). EDA 'Edge Cases': 'California RPP = 110.7 (exact), 1/1, 100%, Spot-check P0 rule: CA RPP BETWEEN 108.0 AND 115.0 (per spec). Verified value already sits at 110.7.' Spec DQ Rules: 'California RPP between 108.0 and 115.0 (P0)'. This is a verified-value rule. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Arkansas (FIPS 05) RPP between 84.0 and 90.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE geo_fips = '05' AND (rpp_all_items < 84.0 OR rpp_all_items > 90.0)
rules.description: Sanity spot-check against a known lowest-cost state. Arkansas's RPP has ranged ~85.6-87.9 over the past decade. The [84.0, 90.0] window covers that history with headroom on both sides. A value outside this window means the row for AR is stale, mis-keyed, or corrupted.
rules.evidence: EDA 'Estimated vs. Verified' table: Arkansas at FIPS 05 is spec-verified at 86.9 (matches BEA release exactly). EDA 'Edge Cases': 'Arkansas RPP = 86.9 (exact), 1/1, 100%, Spot-check P0 rule: AR RPP BETWEEN 84.0 AND 90.0 (per spec). Verified value already sits at 86.9.' Spec DQ Rules: 'Arkansas RPP between 84.0 and 90.0 (P0)'. This is a verified-value rule. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: District of Columbia (FIPS 11) present exactly once
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE geo_fips = '11') = 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: District of Columbia is not a state but BEA includes it in SARPP at FIPS=11. Many state-only filters accidentally drop DC. This rule guards against that regression -- the row count rule (RAW-BEA-001) would still catch a total miscount, but this rule points directly at the DC-specific failure mode.
rules.evidence: EDA 'Key Findings': 'DC (11) is included.' EDA 'Edge Cases': 'DC present at FIPS 11, 1/51, 2.0%, If any rule filters "states only," explicitly include FIPS 11.' Domain context: 'DC included at FIPS 11. Territories excluded.' DC is a spec-verified value (109.9). Threshold: count = 1 exactly.

rules.name: All 51 expected FIPS codes present (canonical set)
rules.description: The canonical set of 51 2-digit FIPS codes for the 50 states + DC. Note intentional gaps at 03, 07, 14, 43, 52 (unassigned FIPS codes). This rule catches silent single-row drops that the row count rule alone would not distinguish from a double-load offset, AND it rejects unexpected FIPS codes (e.g., territories like PR=72, metro-area codes) that happen to land at count 51 by coincidence.
rules.evidence: EDA 'geo_fips' field profile enumerates all 51 expected values: '01, 02, 04, 05, 06, 08, 09, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15-42, 44-51, 53-56'. EDA 'Key Findings': 'All 51 expected FIPS codes are present... note deliberate gaps at 03, 07, 14, 43, 52 which never existed in the FIPS scheme.' EDA 'Edge Cases': 'All 51 expected FIPS present, canonical-51-list, P0, catches silent drops of any single row.' Threshold: all 51 canonical codes resolve, count = 51.

rules.name: geo_fips format: 2-digit zero-padded numeric string
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE NOT regexp_matches(geo_fips, '^[0-9]{2}$')
rules.description: geo_fips must be a 2-character string of exactly two decimal digits (e.g., '06', not '6' or '06.0'). This catches float coercion errors (where '06' becomes 6.0) and zero-padding regressions.
rules.evidence: EDA 'geo_fips' field profile: 'Patterns: ^\d{2}$ -- 51/51 match'. EDA 'Edge Cases': 'geo_fips matches ^\d{2}$, 51/51, 100%, P1 regex rule'. Spec DQ Rules: 'geo_fips format: 2-digit numeric string (P1)'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: source_method in allowed domain {bea_api, csv_cache}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE source_method NOT IN ('bea_api', 'csv_cache')
rules.description: source_method is a provenance signal with exactly two allowed values per spec: 'bea_api' (live BEA API call succeeded) or 'csv_cache' (fell back to cached CSV at data/raw/bea_cache/bea_rpp_2024.csv). The rule uses an IN list rather than an equality because the current load is 100% csv_cache but a future load with a working BEA_API_KEY will be 100% bea_api. Both are valid steady states; neither should be pinned.
rules.evidence: EDA 'source_method' field profile: 'Cardinality: 1 distinct value (csv_cache). 51/51 = csv_cache. Patterns: Expected domain is {bea_api, csv_cache} per spec.' EDA 'Key Findings': 'source_method is uniformly csv_cache -- no row came from the live BEA API in this load. DQ rules must not hard-require bea_api as the method.' EDA explicit 'Do NOT write' warning: 'A rule pinning source_method = bea_api (today's load is 100% csv_cache)'. Spec schema: 'source_method: "bea_api" or "csv_cache"'. Threshold: 0 rows with an out-of-domain source_method.

rules.name: source_url non-null (provenance)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE source_url IS NULL
rules.description: source_url captures the BEA API URL (with API key redacted) that logically sourced this row, regardless of whether the live API call or the CSV fallback was used. Required by schema and by downstream lineage/contract consumers.
rules.evidence: EDA 'source_url' field profile: 'Null Rate: 0%. Cardinality: 1 distinct value (BEA API URL template with UserID=REDACTED). The API key is redacted in the stored URL (good hygiene).' EDA 'Edge Cases' recommended rules table (#13): 'source_url non-null, 0 nulls; provenance column.' Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: load_date non-null (provenance)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE load_date IS NULL
rules.description: load_date is a required provenance column (date of the batch load). This rule enforces non-null only; it deliberately does NOT pin load_date to a specific value because it is a load-batch stamp that changes on every ingest run.
rules.evidence: EDA 'load_date' field profile: 'Null Rate: 0%. Cardinality: 1 distinct value (2026-04-10). Distribution: Constant -- single batch ingest.' EDA explicit 'Do NOT write' warning: 'A rule pinning ingested_at to a fixed value (it is a batch stamp and will change every load)' -- same applies to load_date. EDA 'Edge Cases' recommended rules table (#14): 'load_date non-null, 0 nulls'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: ingested_at non-null (provenance)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: ingested_at is a required provenance column (timestamp of the batch load). Non-null only; never pinned to an exact value because the ingested_at stamp is constant within a load but changes between loads.
rules.evidence: EDA 'ingested_at' field profile: 'Null Rate: 0%. Cardinality: 1 distinct value (2026-04-10 22:13:34.381457). Distribution: Constant -- entire batch stamped identically. Acts as a batch ID, not a per-row event time.' EDA explicit 'Do NOT write' warning: 'A rule pinning ingested_at to a fixed value'. EDA 'Edge Cases' recommended rules table (#15). Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: Freshness: load_date within 400 days
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bea_rpp WHERE load_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '400' DAY LIMIT 1
rules.description: BEA publishes RPPs annually (each February for the prior year). A 400-day freshness window allows one full BEA release cycle of slack before raising a stale-data signal. This is intentionally looser than the 30-day freshness rules used for quarterly sources (College Scorecard, BLS OOH, O*NET) because RPP data has no within-year revisions and no business need for monthly refresh.
rules.evidence: EDA 'Temporal Pattern': 'Annual snapshot; every row carries data_year = 2024 and a single ingested_at timestamp'. Domain context: 'Cadence: Annual snapshot. BEA publishes new RPPs each February for the prior calendar year... There is no within-year variation; a single data_year is the only temporal dimension in the table.' A 400-day window tolerates one full annual cycle (365 days) plus ~5 weeks of slack between publication and load. Threshold: 0 rows older than 400 days.

rules.name: Distribution sanity: mean rpp_all_items within [94.0, 100.0]
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT AVG(rpp_all_items) FROM bronze.bea_rpp) BETWEEN 94.0 AND 100.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: A simple arithmetic mean of all 51 state RPPs should land near but not exactly at the BEA national baseline of 100.0 because the baseline is population-weighted while the simple mean is not. Historically this value sits in the ~96-98 range. The [94.0, 100.0] window tolerates both the current estimates-in-place state (observed 96.98) and a future live-BEA refresh while still catching catastrophic unit errors that a pointwise range rule might miss.
rules.evidence: EDA 'Key Findings': 'Distribution is approximately centered on the national baseline: mean 96.98, median 96.90, std dev 7.06.' EDA 'rpp_all_items' field profile confirms mean = 96.98. EDA recommended rule #16: 'Distribution sanity: mean, abs(mean(rpp_all_items) - 97.0) <= 3.0, P1 (soft), Observed mean 96.98. A future real-BEA load should still center near 97 because simple-average-of-states routinely lands 96-98.' Rule is estimate-tolerant and refresh-tolerant. Threshold: mean in [94.0, 100.0].

rules.name: Distribution sanity: min rpp_all_items >= 84.0
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT MIN(rpp_all_items) FROM bronze.bea_rpp) >= 84.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The cheapest state in BEA's last decade of publications has been Arkansas or Mississippi in the ~85.6-87.9 range. Floor of 84.0 gives ~1.6 point headroom below the historical floor. Complements the pointwise range rule (RAW-BEA-003) by monitoring the tail directly.
rules.evidence: EDA 'rpp_all_items' field profile: 'min = 86.90 (Arkansas)'. EDA recommended rule #17: 'Distribution sanity: min floor, min(rpp_all_items) >= 84.0, P1 (soft), Observed min 86.90 (AR); historical floor ~85.6 over last decade.' Threshold: min >= 84.0.

rules.name: Distribution sanity: max rpp_all_items <= 115.0
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT MAX(rpp_all_items) FROM bronze.bea_rpp) <= 115.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The most expensive state in BEA's last decade has been California (peak ~113 in 2013). Ceiling of 115.0 gives ~2 point headroom above the historical ceiling. Complements RAW-BEA-003 and RAW-BEA-007 by monitoring the upper tail directly.
rules.evidence: EDA 'rpp_all_items' field profile: 'max = 110.70 (California)'. EDA recommended rule #18: 'Distribution sanity: max ceiling, max(rpp_all_items) <= 115.0, P1 (soft), Observed max 110.70 (CA); historical ceiling ~113 (CA 2013 peak) over last decade.' Threshold: max <= 115.0.
